note: Puppeteer screenshots failed (system resource limits); review based on QA-29 screenshots + full source analysis of App.jsx, Office.jsx, App.css, Office.css, and data files. No feature regressions observed in confirmed-working items, but flat-array schema has genuine new bugs.
new_since_last_review: Flat array workers.json format support (partially broken — see HIGH bugs)
Personalized confetti color per worker (Maya in-progress, Carlos/vibe confetti working)
Vibe streak counter with tier icons (working)

suggestions.title: Flat-array workers missing `id` breaks avatars, ghost detection, and React keys
suggestions.description: workers.json flat array has `id` for Sofia but NOT for Maya or Dave. This cascades into three distinct failures: (1) `CharacterAvatar` checks `workerId === 'maya'` etc., but `worker.id` is `undefined` for un-id'd workers — so Maya renders as a bare 🎨 emoji and Dave as 🚀 in the Office view instead of their personalized SVGs. (2) `activeIds = new Set(workers.map(w => w.id))` becomes `{undefined, 'sofia'}`, so Maya and Dave are NOT recognized as active — causing them to appear as ghosts in the roster AND working characters simultaneously. (3) `<Character key={occ.worker.id}/>` produces `key=undefined`, triggering React reconciliation warnings. Fix: ensure all workers in the flat array include an `id` field matching their expected character ID (e.g. `"id": "maya"`).

suggestions.title: `updated_at` vs `started` field mismatch silently breaks duration display and deep-work badge
suggestions.description: workers.json (flat array format) provides a `started` field but Office.jsx and WorkerCard both reference `worker.updated_at`. Specific breakage: (1) WorkerCard's `formatDuration(worker.updated_at)` guard `worker.status === 'working' && worker.updated_at` always fails for flat-array workers → the ⏱️ working duration never renders. (2) Office.jsx `char--deep-work` class check: `worker.updated_at && Date.now() - new Date(worker.updated_at).getTime() > 45 * 60 * 1000` — always false, so the deep-work visual never fires. (3) `ProgressBar` ETA calculation gets `updatedAt={worker.updated_at}` → `undefined` → ETA is never shown. Note StatsBar correctly uses `w.started` — that one works. Fix: either normalize the flat array schema to include `updated_at`, or make the code accept `worker.updated_at ?? worker.started` as the working timestamp.

suggestions.title: ErrorBoundary still absent — 5th flag, escalating to blocking
suggestions.description: No React ErrorBoundary anywhere in the component tree. This has been flagged in reviews #26, #27, #28, #29, and now #30. A data-shape error (e.g. null worker.name, malformed activity.json entry) will crash the entire UI with a blank white screen and zero user feedback. With the new flat-array format introducing nullable fields (Dave has `task: ''`, `started: null`), the blast radius is growing. `new Date(null).getTime()` returns 0 — this means any code path doing date math on `started: null` without a guard will produce wildly incorrect values rather than crashing, which is actually worse (silent garbage). Wrap at minimum the main content area and activity log in an ErrorBoundary that renders a friendly fallback. Effort is genuinely small — it's a 20-line component.

suggestions.title: Activity log worker emojis fall back to 🤖 in flat-array mode
suggestions.description: When workers.json is a flat array, `syncFromGitHub` calls `setRoster([])`. `ActivityLogEntry` resolves worker emoji via `roster?.find(r => r.name === entry.worker)`, which finds nothing when roster is empty → all named workers show 🤖 instead of their real emoji (🎨, 🚀, 🔍, etc.). The flat-array workers DO carry an `emoji` field but it's never plumbed to the activity log lookup. Fix: pass `allWorkers` as a fallback emoji source — e.g. `const workerEmoji = workerData?.emoji ?? allWorkers.find(w => w.name === entry.worker)?.emoji ?? '🤖'`.

suggestions.title: Component extraction still pending — App.jsx is 38 KB, Office.jsx is 28 KB (5th flag)
suggestions.description: App.jsx has grown to 38 KB and now houses: ActivityLogEntry, ActivityLog, StatusBadge, ProgressBar, WorkerCard, DeltaBadge, StatsBar, EmptyState, LoadingSkeleton, and the root App. Office.jsx at 28 KB contains: CharacterAvatar (250 lines of SVG paths), Character, WindowElement, and the Office orchestrator. This monolith makes diffs noisy, increases risk of merge conflicts, and blocks Maya from working on confetti/animations without touching Carlos's data logic. Proposed extraction targets: `src/components/WorkerCard.jsx`, `src/components/ActivityLog.jsx`, `src/components/StatsBar.jsx`, `src/components/CharacterAvatar.jsx`, `src/components/WindowElement.jsx`. The CharacterAvatar SVG alone is 200+ lines and has zero business logic — it belongs in its own file.

suggestions.title: `WORKER_ROLE_COLORS` in App.jsx duplicates and diverges from `ROLE_COLORS` in constants.js
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 14 defines `WORKER_ROLE_COLORS = { Maya: '#a855f7', Carlos: '#3b82f6', Dave: '#f97316', Sofia: '#22c55e', Luna: '#8b5cf6' }` keyed by NAME. constants.js defines `ROLE_COLORS` keyed by ROLE (e.g. 'Frontend Engineer': '#a78bfa'). Not only is this duplication, the actual hex values differ: Maya's confetti fires `#a855f7` (purple-500) but her role color is `#a78bfa` (violet-400). This means the personalized completion confetti doesn't visually match the role accent color used on her card and desk. Consolidate: either key WORKER_ROLE_COLORS off IDs and move it to constants.js, or derive it from ROLE_COLORS via the worker's role string.

suggestions.title: Idle worker with empty `task` field shows hollow task section
suggestions.description:
Dave's workers.json entry has `task: ""`. WorkerCard renders the task block unconditionally: `
📋 Current Task{worker.task}
`. When task is an empty string, this renders the label but nothing beneath it — a visually awkward empty box with just the '📋 Current Task' heading. Same issue would affect any future worker in idle/done status without a task. Fix: either suppress the task block entirely when `!worker.task`, or show a placeholder like 'On standby' / 'No active task'.

suggestions.title: Completion toast uses ~200 chars of inline styles instead of a CSS class
suggestions.description: The `completionToast` element in App.jsx (line ~781) is styled with a large `style` prop object: `position: 'fixed', bottom: '2rem', left: '50%', transform: 'translateX(-50%)'`, plus border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, and animation. Every other transient UI element (fetch-error-banner, speech-bubble, etc.) uses CSS classes. The inline styles make it impossible to override via theme variables and kill light-mode compatibility — the background hardcodes `var(--surface2)` as a string in JS but the box-shadow hardcodes `rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` which is dark-mode only. Extract to `.completion-toast` CSS class, apply theme-aware variables.

suggestions.title: Interactive character divs in Office.jsx lack `role="button"`
suggestions.description: In Office.jsx, the `Character` component renders a `<div>` with `tabIndex={0}`, `onClick`, `onFocus`, and `onBlur` — making it a custom interactive element. Without `role="button"`, screen readers announce these as generic regions, not as activatable controls. VoiceOver and NVDA users would hear 'group' or nothing interactive for clickable characters. Add `role="button"` when `onClick` is defined (i.e., when `handleClick` is set). Also consider adding `onKeyDown` to trigger click on Enter/Space for full keyboard equivalence (currently tabbing to a character and pressing Enter does nothing actionable).

suggestions.title: `prefers-reduced-motion` not applied to App.css animations
suggestions.description: Office.css has a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block that disables character/plant animations and a separate block for the window. App.css has a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block only for `.live-dot`. Missing: `card-rise`, `new-border-pulse`, `confetti-piece`, `ambient-particle`, `worker-card--focused` bounce, and `progress-milestone-*` flash animations. For users with vestibular disorders, 30 confetti pieces flying and cards bouncing in on every data refresh is genuinely problematic. Add a comprehensive reduce-motion block in App.css similar to what Office.css already does.

suggestions.title: Activity log silently drops events beyond 20 with no load-more affordance
suggestions.description: ActivityLog does `[...entries].reverse().slice(0, 20)` with no pagination. The current activity.json has enough entries that users can't see older history at all. As the log grows (especially with high-frequency syncs and team activity), the cutoff becomes more impactful. The 'Last N events' counter in the section header accurately tells users they're seeing a subset, which is good, but there's no way to see more. A simple 'Load more' button or expanding accordion for the 'Older' time group would resolve this with minimal effort.
